# Función para generar los datos sintéticos
gen_od_pom <- function(
  n = 500,
  n_cont_norm = 5,
  n_cont_skew = 2,
  n_bin = 2,
  n_fact = 2,
  fact_levels = c(3, 4),
  rho = 0.5,
  n_latent = 0,
  latent_strength = 0.0,
  beta_sd = 0.5,
  n_levels_y = 5,
  thresholds = NULL,
  nonlinear_frac = 0.0,
  nonlinear_strength = 0.0,
  include_interactions = FALSE,
  interaction_types = c("xN:xN"),
  interaction_frac = 0.1,
  interaction_sd = 0.25,
  fixed_interactions = NULL,
  fit_with_interactions = TRUE,
  seed = NULL
) {
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 0. Comprobaciones iniciales
  # ==========================================================
  
  if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)
  
  if (!requireNamespace("MASS", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("El paquete 'MASS' es necesario. Instálalo con install.packages('MASS').")
  }
  
  if (n_levels_y < 3) {
    stop("n_levels_y debe ser al menos 3 para una respuesta ordinal útil.")
  }
  
  if (n_fact > 0 && length(fact_levels) < n_fact) {
    stop("fact_levels debe tener al menos n_fact elementos.")
  }
  
  if (latent_strength < 0 || latent_strength > 1) {
    stop("latent_strength debe estar entre 0 y 1.")
  }
  
  if (nonlinear_frac < 0 || nonlinear_frac > 1) {
    stop("nonlinear_frac debe estar entre 0 y 1.")
  }
  
  if (interaction_frac < 0 || interaction_frac > 1) {
    stop("interaction_frac debe estar entre 0 y 1.")
  }
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 1. Función auxiliar: matriz de correlación AR(1)
  # ==========================================================
  
  make_ar1_cor <- function(p, rho) {
    if (p <= 1) return(matrix(1, nrow = p, ncol = p))
    outer(1:p, 1:p, function(i, j) rho^abs(i - j))
  }
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 2. Generar variables continuas normales correlacionadas
  # ==========================================================
  
  X_list <- list()
  
  if (n_cont_norm > 0) {
    Sigma <- make_ar1_cor(n_cont_norm, rho)
    
    X_norm <- MASS::mvrnorm(
      n = n,
      mu = rep(0, n_cont_norm),
      Sigma = Sigma
    )
    
    X_norm <- scale(X_norm)
    X_norm <- as.data.frame(X_norm)
    names(X_norm) <- paste0("xN", seq_len(n_cont_norm))
    
    X_list[["norm"]] <- X_norm
  }
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 3. Generar variables continuas sesgadas tipo lognormal
  # ==========================================================
  
  if (n_cont_skew > 0) {
    X_skew <- matrix(
      rlnorm(n * n_cont_skew, meanlog = 0, sdlog = 0.7),
      nrow = n,
      ncol = n_cont_skew
    )
    
    X_skew <- scale(X_skew)
    X_skew <- as.data.frame(X_skew)
    names(X_skew) <- paste0("xS", seq_len(n_cont_skew))
    
    X_list[["skew"]] <- X_skew
  }
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 4. Generar variables binarias
  # ==========================================================
  
  if (n_bin > 0) {
    X_bin <- as.data.frame(
      replicate(n_bin, rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.5))
    )
    
    names(X_bin) <- paste0("xB", seq_len(n_bin))
    
    for (j in seq_len(n_bin)) {
      X_bin[[j]] <- factor(X_bin[[j]], levels = c(0, 1))
    }
    
    X_list[["bin"]] <- X_bin
  }
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 5. Generar factores nominales
  # ==========================================================
  
  if (n_fact > 0) {
    X_fact <- data.frame(row_id_tmp = seq_len(n))
    
    for (j in seq_len(n_fact)) {
      lev_j <- fact_levels[j]
      
      X_fact[[paste0("xF", j)]] <- factor(
        sample(seq_len(lev_j), size = n, replace = TRUE),
        levels = seq_len(lev_j)
      )
    }
    
    X_fact$row_id_tmp <- NULL
    
    X_list[["fact"]] <- X_fact
  }
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 6. Combinar predictores
  # ==========================================================
  
  if (length(X_list) == 0) {
    stop("Debe existir al menos un predictor.")
  }
  
  X <- do.call(cbind, X_list)
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 7. Añadir estructura latente opcional
  # ==========================================================
  # La idea es introducir dependencia adicional entre predictores
  # continuos mediante factores latentes comunes.
  
  cont_names <- names(X)[sapply(X, is.numeric)]
  
  if (n_latent > 0 && latent_strength > 0 && length(cont_names) > 0) {
    
    Z_lat <- matrix(
      rnorm(n * n_latent),
      nrow = n,
      ncol = n_latent
    )
    
    loadings <- matrix(
      rnorm(length(cont_names) * n_latent),
      nrow = length(cont_names),
      ncol = n_latent
    )
    
    latent_component <- Z_lat %*% t(loadings)
    latent_component <- scale(latent_component)
    
    for (j in seq_along(cont_names)) {
      x_old <- scale(X[[cont_names[j]]])
      x_new <- sqrt(1 - latent_strength^2) * x_old +
        latent_strength * latent_component[, j]
      
      X[[cont_names[j]]] <- as.numeric(scale(x_new))
    }
  }
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 8. Construir términos no lineales opcionales
  # ==========================================================
  # La no linealidad entra en el mecanismo generador de y,
  # no necesariamente en el modelo ajustado.
  
  nonlinear_vars <- character(0)
  nonlinear_terms <- NULL
  gamma_nl <- NULL
  
  cont_names <- names(X)[sapply(X, is.numeric)]
  
  if (nonlinear_frac > 0 && nonlinear_strength > 0 && length(cont_names) > 0) {
    
    n_nl <- max(1, round(nonlinear_frac * length(cont_names)))
    nonlinear_vars <- sample(cont_names, size = n_nl, replace = FALSE)
    
    nonlinear_terms <- sapply(nonlinear_vars, function(v) {
      z <- as.numeric(scale(X[[v]]))
      as.numeric(scale(z^2))
    })
    
    if (is.null(dim(nonlinear_terms))) {
      nonlinear_terms <- matrix(nonlinear_terms, ncol = 1)
    }
    
    colnames(nonlinear_terms) <- paste0(nonlinear_vars, "_sq")
    
    gamma_nl <- rnorm(
      n = ncol(nonlinear_terms),
      mean = 0,
      sd = nonlinear_strength
    )
  }
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 9. Construir fórmula base
  # ==========================================================
  
  base_terms <- names(X)
  formula_base <- as.formula(
    paste("y_ord ~", paste(base_terms, collapse = " + "))
  )
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 10. Crear posibles interacciones
  # ==========================================================
  
  interaction_terms <- character(0)
  interaction_beta <- NULL
  
  numeric_vars <- names(X)[sapply(X, is.numeric)]
  bin_vars <- names(X)[sapply(X, function(z) is.factor(z) && nlevels(z) == 2)]
  fact_vars <- names(X)[sapply(X, function(z) is.factor(z) && nlevels(z) > 2)]
  
  candidate_interactions <- character(0)
  
  if (include_interactions) {
    
    if ("xN:xN" %in% interaction_types && length(numeric_vars) >= 2) {
      combs <- combn(numeric_vars, 2)
      candidate_interactions <- c(
        candidate_interactions,
        apply(combs, 2, paste, collapse = ":")
      )
    }
    
    if ("xN:xB" %in% interaction_types &&
        length(numeric_vars) >= 1 &&
        length(bin_vars) >= 1) {
      combs <- expand.grid(numeric_vars, bin_vars)
      candidate_interactions <- c(
        candidate_interactions,
        paste(combs[, 1], combs[, 2], sep = ":")
      )
    }
    
    if ("xN:xF" %in% interaction_types &&
        length(numeric_vars) >= 1 &&
        length(fact_vars) >= 1) {
      combs <- expand.grid(numeric_vars, fact_vars)
      candidate_interactions <- c(
        candidate_interactions,
        paste(combs[, 1], combs[, 2], sep = ":")
      )
    }
    
    if (!is.null(fixed_interactions)) {
      candidate_interactions <- unique(c(candidate_interactions, fixed_interactions))
    }
    
    candidate_interactions <- unique(candidate_interactions)
    
    if (length(candidate_interactions) > 0) {
      
      if (!is.null(fixed_interactions)) {
        fixed_ok <- fixed_interactions[
          fixed_interactions %in% candidate_interactions
        ]
      } else {
        fixed_ok <- character(0)
      }
      
      remaining <- setdiff(candidate_interactions, fixed_ok)
      
      n_random_int <- max(
        0,
        round(interaction_frac * length(candidate_interactions)) - length(fixed_ok)
      )
      
      random_int <- if (length(remaining) > 0 && n_random_int > 0) {
        sample(
          remaining,
          size = min(n_random_int, length(remaining)),
          replace = FALSE
        )
      } else {
        character(0)
      }
      
      interaction_terms <- unique(c(fixed_ok, random_int))
    }
  }
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 11. Fórmula para el modelo generador
  # ==========================================================
  
  if (length(interaction_terms) > 0) {
    formula_gen <- as.formula(
      paste(
        "y_ord ~",
        paste(c(base_terms, interaction_terms), collapse = " + ")
      )
    )
  } else {
    formula_gen <- formula_base
  }
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 12. Crear matriz de diseño para el predictor lineal
  # ==========================================================
  
  X_for_mm <- X
  X_for_mm$y_ord <- ordered(rep(1, n), levels = seq_len(n_levels_y))
  
  X_mm <- model.matrix(formula_gen, data = X_for_mm)
  
  # Quitamos intercepto.
  X_no_intercept <- X_mm[, colnames(X_mm) != "(Intercept)", drop = FALSE]
  
  p <- ncol(X_no_intercept)
  
  beta_true <- rnorm(
    n = p,
    mean = 0,
    sd = beta_sd
  )
  
  names(beta_true) <- colnames(X_no_intercept)
  
  eta_linear <- as.vector(X_no_intercept %*% beta_true)
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 13. Añadir no linealidad al predictor lineal
  # ==========================================================
  
  eta_nonlinear <- rep(0, n)
  
  if (!is.null(nonlinear_terms) && !is.null(gamma_nl)) {
    eta_nonlinear <- as.vector(nonlinear_terms %*% gamma_nl)
  }
  
  eta <- eta_linear + eta_nonlinear
  
  # Centramos eta para estabilizar la generación ordinal.
  eta <- as.numeric(scale(eta, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE))
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 14. Definir puntos de corte ordinales
  # ==========================================================
  
  if (is.null(thresholds)) {
    # Umbrales simétricos razonables.
    # Para 5 niveles: aprox. -1.5, -0.5, 0.5, 1.5
    thresholds <- seq(
      from = -1.5,
      to = 1.5,
      length.out = n_levels_y - 1
    )
  }
  
  if (length(thresholds) != n_levels_y - 1) {
    stop("thresholds debe tener longitud n_levels_y - 1.")
  }
  
  if (any(diff(thresholds) <= 0)) {
    stop("Los thresholds deben estar estrictamente ordenados de menor a mayor.")
  }
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 15. Calcular probabilidades acumuladas P(Y <= j)
  # ==========================================================
  # Modelo:
  # logit[P(Y <= j)] = theta_j - eta
  
  cum_probs <- sapply(thresholds, function(th) {
    plogis(th - eta)
  })
  
  if (is.null(dim(cum_probs))) {
    cum_probs <- matrix(cum_probs, ncol = 1)
  }
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 16. Convertir probabilidades acumuladas en probabilidades
  #     por categoría
  # ==========================================================
  
  probs <- matrix(NA, nrow = n, ncol = n_levels_y)
  
  probs[, 1] <- cum_probs[, 1]
  
  if (n_levels_y > 2) {
    for (j in 2:(n_levels_y - 1)) {
      probs[, j] <- cum_probs[, j] - cum_probs[, j - 1]
    }
  }
  
  probs[, n_levels_y] <- 1 - cum_probs[, n_levels_y - 1]
  
  # Corrección numérica por seguridad.
  probs[probs < 0] <- 0
  probs <- probs / rowSums(probs)
  
  colnames(probs) <- paste0("P_Y", seq_len(n_levels_y))
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 17. Simular respuesta ordinal
  # ==========================================================
  
  y_ord_num <- apply(probs, 1, function(pr) {
    sample(seq_len(n_levels_y), size = 1, prob = pr)
  })
  
  y_ord <- ordered(
    y_ord_num,
    levels = seq_len(n_levels_y)
  )
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 18. Construir base final
  # ==========================================================
  
  df <- X
  df$y_ord <- y_ord
  
  # Ponemos la respuesta primero, como en tu estructura habitual.
  df <- df[, c("y_ord", setdiff(names(df), "y_ord"))]
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 19. Ajustar modelo POM
  # ==========================================================
  
  if (fit_with_interactions && length(interaction_terms) > 0) {
    formula_fit <- formula_gen
  } else {
    formula_fit <- formula_base
  }
  
  fit_pom <- tryCatch(
    MASS::polr(
      formula = formula_fit,
      data = df,
      method = "logistic",
      Hess = TRUE
    ),
    error = function(e) {
      warning("El modelo polr no pudo ajustarse: ", e$message)
      return(NULL)
    }
  )
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 20. Resumen de la distribución ordinal
  # ==========================================================
  
  y_distribution <- data.frame(
    level = levels(df$y_ord),
    n = as.vector(table(df$y_ord)),
    prop = as.vector(prop.table(table(df$y_ord)))
  )
  
  
  # ==========================================================
  # 21. Salida
  # ==========================================================
  
  out <- list(
    data = df,
    fit_pom = fit_pom,
    formula_gen = formula_gen,
    formula_fit = formula_fit,
    truth = list(
      beta_true = beta_true,
      thresholds = thresholds,
      eta = eta,
      eta_linear = eta_linear,
      eta_nonlinear = eta_nonlinear,
      nonlinear_vars = nonlinear_vars,
      gamma_nonlinear = gamma_nl,
      interaction_terms = interaction_terms,
      n_levels_y = n_levels_y,
      probs = probs,
      y_distribution = y_distribution
    ),
    settings = list(
      n = n,
      n_cont_norm = n_cont_norm,
      n_cont_skew = n_cont_skew,
      n_bin = n_bin,
      n_fact = n_fact,
      fact_levels = fact_levels,
      rho = rho,
      n_latent = n_latent,
      latent_strength = latent_strength,
      beta_sd = beta_sd,
      n_levels_y = n_levels_y,
      thresholds = thresholds,
      nonlinear_frac = nonlinear_frac,
      nonlinear_strength = nonlinear_strength,
      include_interactions = include_interactions,
      interaction_types = interaction_types,
      interaction_frac = interaction_frac,
      interaction_sd = interaction_sd,
      fixed_interactions = fixed_interactions,
      fit_with_interactions = fit_with_interactions,
      seed = seed
    )
  )
  
  class(out) <- "gen_od_pom"
  
  return(out)
}
